name: What proof do I need for my repetitive strain injury claim from my desk job?
description: Doctor reports, work logs, witnesses tying to job duties.[1][2]

mainEntity.name: What proof do I need for my repetitive strain injury claim from my desk job?

acceptedAnswer.text: Doctor reports, work logs, witnesses tying to job duties.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description: Medical reports linking injury to work duties, incident details, witness statements, and wage records; doctor’s Form 123 is key.[1][2] Utah covers occupational illnesses like repetitive strain if work-related.[1] Key factors: Timeline of symptoms; exclusion of non-work causes; prompt reporting. Key factors: Physician confirmation of work causation.[2] Practical guidance: Keep work logs/hours; avoid gaps in treatment. Submit everything to insurer early. Evidence builds stronger compensability case.[1]
